Patch Set 7:

Fairly extensive changes cleanup in this patch set.

- Incorporate improvements suggested by Andreas.
- Use \a instead of \param where appropriate.
- Fix a few other doxygen errors.
- Capitalize acronyms (LDLM, AST, RPC, UUID, MDT, etc.)
- Fix various formatting and language issues in comments.
- Conserve vertical space by not putting /* and */ on separate lines for comments within function bodies.
- Add lock compatibility matrix and lock type descriptions to lustre_dlm.h.
- Please review changes in ldlm_prepare_lru_list() for accuracy.
- Rebase on tip of master.

Patch Set 12: (4 inline comments)

If Oleg is requesting the patch be updated again, I'd like to (politely :-) ask if the "in-between lines" are also converted to tabs.

Since this patch is changing so many lines in the ldlm files, it is a great opportunity to also do a lot of tab conversion.  That won't increase the "patch conflict surface area", but it will help convert a lot of lines to tabs that are otherwise kind of left ragged with alternating lines of tabs and spaces.  The policy is up to +/- 6 lines between two modified lines, or +/- 3 lines at the start/end of a function/structure, but you are free to use your own discretion to increase this if it finishes off a whole code block or function or structure.

I did this in another huge patch via "git format-patch HEAD~1" to get a patch file, and edited the patch directly (if you are comfortable with that sort of thing) in vim with a search for tabs highlighting where they are missing, then reset the branch to the previous commit via "git reset --hard HEAD~1", and re-imported the edited patch via "git am 0001-whatever-the-patch-is-called.patch".

If editing patches by hand isn't your cup of tea, just "git show > tt" highlighting the tabs shows where there are small clusters of in-between lines that are not using tabs, and then the actual file can be edited, and "git commit -a --amend" to merge the changes into the patch.

Patch Set 12:

Andreas, I actually ran my patch through a perl script that I *thought* caught all non-tab-converted "in-between" lines using the 6-line rule.  Did you find cases that I missed in your review, or is this just a general reminder?  Thanks

Patch Set 12:

parents.subject: LU-1994 kernel: 3.6 dentry_open uses struct path as first arg

commit.subject: LU-1914 ldlm: add doxygen comments
commit.message:
LU-1914 ldlm: add doxygen comments

Update original patch from bugzilla b=20943 written by Oleg Drokin for
master.  Extensively document functions, data structures and
subsystems in the ldlm module.  Make various improvements to language
and formatting of existing comments.  Complete conversion of major
struct definitions to tab indentation.

Signed-off-by: Ned Bass <bass6@llnl.gov>
Oracle-bug-id: b=20943
Change-Id: I10086a1be943b2b473f90129bc2d6abab31d9bd7
